rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCELLA PIERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) In her second report of status, filed on 12/6/24, Administrator had reported that the tenants had been evicted "in October." She is still alleging this a year later. The real property does not need to be sold to be distributed to the heirs. (3) If the real property has not been sold, there was no loss "on sale," which is the loss reported in a probate accounting. Diminution in the fair market value should be reported on an informational schedule. [Probate Code Section 1063(a).] Please provide a courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/27/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HURBERT LEE DONALDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) The allegation on the petition seems contradictory as to whether the decedent died testate or intestate. (2) The examiner is unable to complete the review because this petition is missing information; not all sections have been filled out. (3) Additionally, other required documents, such as the original will, notice, proposed order, and duties and liabilities, are not on file. The Court will decide whether to dismiss this petition. (4) The supplement filed by the petitioner on 3/3/2026 does not support the request to admit the decedent's will to probate or to appoint the petitioner as the personal representative of the estate. (5) Further review will be done when all necessary documents are on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY THERESA DAVIS LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Trustee To Provide Copy Of Trust And Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/09/2026, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner on 03/13/2026) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) Notice must be given to the trustee and all known beneficiaries on the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified. 4) The petition must state the trustee's address, together with the names and addresses of all known beneficiaries of the trust. See CRC 7.902. 5) The petition must identify the proposed successor trustee in the event of the current trustee's removal or suspension. The proposed successor trustee's signed consent to act and give a bond if required must also be filed. 6) A petition to compel the trustee to account cannot be filed until at least 60 days elapse after written request of the beneficiary. See Probate Code section 17200(b)(7)(C). In addition, the following issue must be addressed: The supplement filed on 03/16/2026 (presumably to clear one of the issues) must be verified.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANITA JANE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 7 of the petition must be completed when a decedent is not survived by a spouse or issue. (2) Was nephew William Thomas Addison not survived by issue? (3) Was Decedent's brother Thomas Macklin Williams not survived by issue? Please provide a courtesy copy to the assigned examiner of the supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, by 5/27/26. If there are other interested parties, proof of service of notice to them of the 6/10/26 hearing must also be filed and a courtesy copy provided to the examiner by 5/27/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It would be really helpful if courtesy copies were provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. Lack of courtesy copies makes reviewing cases more difficult.)
